news_college_hockey.title: Final Bracket Projection: Will the committee deviate from past precedent?
news_college_hockey.author: Mike McMahon
news_college_hockey.summary:
Alright, here we go. The NCAA will officially unveil its bracket on ESPN-U today at 3 p.m., and with it, the final word on a season that has been anything but predictable.

Let’s dig into the final projection, and there are some interesting things that the committee might have to consider.

First, the final NPI:

  1. Michigan [AQ]

  2. North Dakota

  3. Michigan State

  4. Western Michigan

  5. Denver [AQ]

  6. Dartmouth [AQ]

  7. Providence

  8. Minnesota Duluth

  9. Penn State

  10. Quinnipiac

  11. Cornell

  12. Wisconsin

  13. Minnesota State [AQ]

  14. Connecticut

  15. Merrimack [AQ]

  16. Bentley [AQ]

On paper, a straight serpentine bracket would slot teams accordingly.

The conventional approach places No. 1 seeds closest to home. But this bracket has a wrinkle, and it starts with Denver.

As a host in Loveland, Denver is locked into that regional. That has a ripple effect, particularly on Michigan State and Western Michigan. Both are No. 1 seeds, and both are headed on the road regardless. Their placement likely hinged on Denver.

Read more

news_college_hockey.title: What I'm hearing on where things stand in Minnesota's coaching search
news_college_hockey.author: Mike McMahon
news_college_hockey.summary:
Minnesota is moving with purpose in its search for a new head coach.

Just days after announcing that Bob Motzko won’t return, the Gophers have already conducted multiple virtual interviews. The process has accelerated quickly, and all indications are that it has narrowed to a short list of finalists.

This newsletter (#664) lays out everything known about the search — who Minnesota has contacted, where things stand, and what comes next.

Let’s start with the headline.

It appears Minnesota has zeroed in on Brett Larson and Grant Potulny as its two finalists. Mick Hatten reported that earlier today, and sources tell College Hockey Insider they’ve heard the same.

There have been other names connected to the search, though. Before diving deeper into the finalists — and acknowledging that these searches can always expand — it’s worth noting one candidate who is no longer in the mix.

Former Minnesota assistant and current Augustana head coach Garrett Raboin has removed himself from consideration, sources told College Hockey Insider on Friday night. Augustana athletic director Josh Morton posted a photo with Raboin that same night, captioned: “Our coach. Now and for years to come — LET’S GO!”

Read more

news_college_hockey.title: Saturday Morning NPI Update: Wisconsin, UConn, and Augustana eyeing the last at-large spots
news_college_hockey.author: Mike McMahon
news_college_hockey.summary:
Minnesota State became the first program to secure a conference championship with a win over St. Thomas on Friday night. The Mavericks punched their ticket back to the NCAA Tournament as the CCHA’s automatic qualifier, continuing a run that has become familiar this time of year.

Positioning remains fluid, however. Minnesota State sits at No. 14 in the NPI entering the final night of conference championship play, with a path to climb to the No. 13 overall seed depending on how results break across the country.

With just one night left to shape the bracket, the NCAA Tournament picture — and the NPI that drives it — remains very much in motion.

This edition of the newsletter (#663) breaks down all of the scenarios.

Wisconsin is on the verge of locking up an at-large berth. The Badgers, UConn, and Augustana are all alive, but it will depend on who wins the automatic qualifiers elsewhere.

Tournament Locks

First, let’s look at the NCAA Tournament locks. I’ll include where these teams could potentially finish overall as well.

  1. Michigan (locked as the No. 1 seed)

  2. North Dakota (locked as the No. 2 seed)

  3. Michigan State (locked as the No. 3 seed)

  4. Western Michigan (locked as the No. 4 seed)

  5. Denver (No. 5-6)

  6. Minnesota Duluth (No. 5-8, but cannot be No. 6)

  7. Dartmouth (No. 6-8)

  8. Providence (No. 6-8)

  9. Penn State (No. 9-10)

  10. Quinnipiac (No. 9-10)

  11. Cornell (locked as the No. 11 seed)

As I wrote earlier this week, we can reasonably project Michigan as the No. 1 seed in Albany and North Dakota as the No. 1 seed in Sioux Falls. The placement of Michigan State and Western Michigan, however, may ultimately hinge on where Denver lands as either the No. 5 or No. 6 overall seed.

The Pioneers are slotted for Loveland as the host school. If they come in as the No. 5 seed, the bracket naturally aligns them with No. 4 Western Michigan in the opposite semifinal. If they slide to No. 6, they would instead draw No. 3 Michigan State.

Read more

news_college_hockey.title: Where things stand with the NPI and the bracket heading into conference championship weekend
news_college_hockey.author: Mike McMahon
news_college_hockey.summary:
Conference championship weekend drops the puck tomorrow, and the picture is coming into sharp focus.

Heading into the weekend, today’s newsletter (#662) is fully devoted to breaking down the current state of the NPI, sorting through the bubble, and mapping out the potential placement scenarios for the NCAA Tournament bracket.

If you have ideas for a future newsletter, tips to pass along, or questions for a mailbag, I’d love to hear from you. You can reach me anytime at mike@collegehockeyinsider.com. Sponsorship inquiries are also welcome.

You can also find me on X at @MikeMcMahonCHN.

And a sincere thank you to our premium subscribers — your support makes independent, in-depth college hockey coverage possible.

Now, let’s get into today’s newsletter.

Before we get into a projected bracket, let’s take a closer look at the NPI and establish what we can definitively say at this point.

The No. 1 Seeds Are Set in Stone

Not only are the four No. 1 seeds locked in, but their exact positions — one through four — are already determined:

  1. Michigan

  2. North Dakota

  3. Michigan State

  4. Western Michigan

Three of those four programs are idle this weekend after being eliminated last week, and Michigan cannot fall below North Dakota even with a loss in the Big Ten championship game.

There’s no intrigue here. The top of the bracket is settled.

Who Is Locked Into the NCAA Tournament?

Entering the weekend, 11 of the 16 NCAA Tournament spots are secured. Wisconsin’s odds are overwhelmingly favorable — if not mathematically certain — and the Atlantic Hockey champion will claim an automatic bid. That effectively brings the number of near-certainties to 13.

Read more

news_ktn.author: 0100019d40f50457-1c17985a-8a06-43aa-b564-bb6108db2ef0-000000@email.washingtonpost.com (0100019d40f50457-1c17985a-8a06-43aa-b564-bb6108db2ef0-000000@email.washingtonpost.com)
news_ktn.summary:
Alert from The Washington Post

The move comes after The Washington Post reported that FBI Director Kash Patel was preparing to release investigative files related to the California Democrat. The Republican-led House Ethics Committee closed a two-year investigation into Swalwell in 2023 without taking any action.

Breaking News

March 30, 6:53 p.m. EDT
 

Rep. Eric Swalwell sends cease-and-desist letter to FBI to avoid release of files about purported ties to suspected Chinese spy

The move comes after The Washington Post reported that FBI Director Kash Patel was preparing to release investigative files related to the California Democrat. The Republican-led House Ethics Committee closed a two-year investigation into Swalwell in 2023 without taking any action.

news_ktn.title: Welcome to the country's national, local and global newsroom.
news_ktn.author: bounce-B5TZXMMYDBYEXKJNVQK4FIBPRI.60257@bounce.nl.npr.org (bounce-B5TZXMMYDBYEXKJNVQK4FIBPRI.60257@bounce.nl.npr.org)
news_ktn.summary:
Please choose a local station
To view this email as a web page, go here.
View this email online
&&&&&&&&&&&&&&

When you connect with your local NPR station, you’re not just forging a bond with one station – you’re joining something bigger. 
 
Meet the NPR Network:
 
3,600 journalists working across 248 newsrooms. 
With a presence in every corner of the country, we’re never far from where a story’s unfolding. And, by extension, neither are you.

Telling the story of the whole country, from everywhere in the country. 
99% of the U.S. population is able to listen to one or more public radio stations, free of charge. Because everyone deserves access to quality information, regardless of their ability to pay.  

National everywhere. Local everywhere. 
We bring you closer to the people, events, politics and cultures that make your home unique, while also giving you access to NPR’s award-winning journalism and podcasts. And don’t forget the stories you get from NPR reporters around the world (because we’re global, too).
 
Mission-driven.
We represent thousands of different perspectives, but we’re united by one goal: to create a more informed public. Without a profit motive behind our work, this mission has been our driving force since day one. It’s why we remain independent and how we’re able to reflect the world as it really is.
 
To get the most from the NPR Network, download the NPR app and choose a local station. The more you listen, the more tailor-made your NPR mix becomes. You’ll get a blend of local, national and global news you can’t get anywhere else.

You’ve started to get to know us. Now, let us get to know you.
Get the app and choose a station
